title: viola ferreyrae
intro: Viola ferreyrae, also known as the Peruvian violet or violet of the Incas, is a perennial flowering plant native to the Andes Mountains in South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which have been found to help soothe digestive issues and reduce fever. The health benefits of Viola ferreyrae include its ability to support immune function, reduce stress and anxiety, and promote better sleep. Viola ferreyrae can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, making it a versatile herbal remedy that can be easily incorporated into a wellness routine.
